# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Distribute Artifacts

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: '16'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Build application
      run: npm run build

    - name: Upload build artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
      with:
        name: build-artifacts
        path: |
          dist/**
          config/**
          scripts/**

  distribute:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build

    steps:
    - name: Download build artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v3
      with:
        name: build-artifacts
        path: ./artifacts

    - name: Securely distribute artifacts
      run: |
        echo "Distributing artifacts securely"
        # Implement secure distribution logic here
        # Ensure all operations are authenticated and authorized

    - name: Clean up
      run: rm -rf ./artifacts

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: none
  id-token: write

env:
  NODE_ENV: production

secrets:
  # Ensure secrets are stored securely in GitHub Secrets
  # and accessed via environment variables or secret inputs
  DISTRIBUTION_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DISTRIBUTION_TOKEN }}
